entry.title: New paper available in Scientific Reports

link.title: New paper available in Scientific Reports

entry.content: My paper Combining Graph Neural Networks and Spatio-temporal Disease Models to Predict COVID-19 Cases in Germany with David Rügamer and Emilio Dorigatti accepted in Scientific Reports is now available online follow this link).

entry.title: Two papers accepted and a new paper on ArXiv

link.title: Two papers accepted and a new paper on ArXiv

entry.content: Two papers I was involved on just got accepted Combining Graph Neural Networks and Spatio-temporal Disease Models to Predict COVID-19 Cases in Germany with David Rügamer and Emilio Dorigatti was just accepted in Scientific Reports. The paper is in press but already available on ArXiv. Additionally, a paper written in german was accepted in Das Gesundheutswesen (similarily the article is only available in press but will soon be online). Finally, a new working paper is out on ArXiv about modelling large bipartite networks (see this link).
entry.summary: One paper got accepted in *Scientific Reports* and one in *Gesundheitswesen*

entry.title: New Preprint on ArXiv about the Statistical modeling of COVID-19 data

link.title: New Preprint on ArXiv about the Statistical modeling of COVID-19 data

entry.content: My paper Statistical modelling of COVID-19 data: Putting Generalised Additive Models to work with quite many people (among them Giacomo De Nicola, Helmut Küchenhoff and Göran Kauermann) is now available on ArXiv.
entry.summary: Statistical modelling of COVID-19 data: Putting Generalised Additive Models to work

entry.title: Paper accepted at International Interactions
entry.content: My paper The Role of Governmental Weapons Procurements in Forecasting Monthly Fatalities in Intrastate Conflicts: A Semiparametric Hierarchical Hurdle Model with Marius Mehrl, Paul W. Thurner and Göran Kauermann was just accepted in International Interactions. The paper is in press but already available on ArXiv.
entry.summary: The Role of Governmental Weapons Procurements in Forecasting Monthly Fatalities in Intrastate Conflicts: A Semiparametric Hierarchical Hurdle Model

entry.title: New Preprint on ArXiv about Modeling Relational Events with Measurement Errors

link.title: New Preprint on ArXiv about Modeling Relational Events with Measurement Errors

entry.content: My paper All that Glitters is not Gold: Modeling Relational Events with Measurement Errors with Marius Mehrl, Paul W. Thurner and Göran Kauermann is now available on ArXiv.
entry.summary: All that Glitters is not Gold: Modeling Relational Events with Measurement Errors

entry.title: Paper accepted at Network Science
entry.content: Our paper Separable and semiparametric network-based counting processes applied to the international combat aircraft trades with Paul W. Thurner and Göran Kauermann is now available available open access in Network Science.
entry.summary: Separable and semiparametric network-based counting processes applied to the international combat aircraft trades

entry.title: Paper accepted at JRSSA
entry.content: My paper * On the Interplay of Regional Mobility, Social Connectedness, and the Spread of COVID-19 in Germany * with Göran Kauermann was just accepted in the Journal of the Royal Statistical Society, Series A. The paper is available online here.
entry.summary: On the Interplay of Regional Mobility, Social Connectedness, and the Spread of COVID-19 in Germany

entry.title: Interview with Center for Data Innovation

link.title: Interview with Center for Data Innovation

entry.content: Hodan Omaar interviewed me on how me and my collegues use Facebook’s Data for Good Program in our work. See the papers On the Interplay of Regional Mobility, Social Connectedness, and the Spread of COVID-19 in Germany *](https://arxiv.org/abs/2008.03013) and [Combining Graph Neural Networks and Spatio-temporal Disease Models to Predict COVID-19 Cases in Germany* for the resulting articles from this collaboration.
entry.summary: 5 Q’s for Cornelius Fritz, Statistician at the University of Munich about Facebook’s Data for Good Program

entry.title: Paper accepted at Tierärztliche Praxis Ausgabe K

link.title: Paper accepted at Tierärztliche Praxis Ausgabe K

entry.content: The paper Food antigen-specific IgE in dogs with suspected food hypersensitivity I wrote with Sandra Baumann and Ralf Müller is now available online!
entry.summary: Food antigen-specific IgE in dogs with suspected food hypersensitivity

entry.title: Paper accepted at Statistica Neerlandica
entry.content: My paper Tempus volat, hora fugit: A survey of tie‐oriented dynamic network models in discrete and continuous time with Michael Lebacher and Göran Kauermann was just accepted for publication in Statistica Neerlandica. The paper is available access here!
entry.summary: Tempus volat, hora fugit: A survey of tie‐oriented dynamic network models in discrete and continuous time
